query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Current SPR level (EIA WCSSTUS1):** **325,655 thousand barrels (~325.7 MB)** for the week ending **June 26, 2026** — the lowest since the early 1980s, down 1.7% w/w and 19.1% y/y.

**Trajectory over the past 3–6 months:**
- **Plateau/peak:** SPR topped out at **415,442 thousand barrels** in early-mid March 2026 (culmination of the 2025 refill); refilling was halted in February when the US–Iran conflict began.
- **Sustained drawdown:** Continuous releases began the week of **March 27, 2026** and ran for ~13 consecutive weeks through June 26.
- **Pace:** Draws accelerated to a **record ~9.9 MB** (week ending May 15) and ran ~7–10 MB/week from late April through mid-June, decelerating to −5.5 MB in the latest week.
- **Magnitude:** ~**89.8 MB** drained from the March peak (~86 MB since December 2025).

**Policy context:** This is an emergency release tied to the US–Iran war / Strait of Hormuz disruption. The US pledged **172 MB** and had released roughly half (~89 MB) by June 26. Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced a plan to refill ~40 MB *after* the conflict ends — not yet reflected in the data.

Data was sourced directly from EIA (data browser + table1.csv), cross-checked with YCharts and multiple news wires. Note: FRED's mirror of WCSSTUS1 was not retrievable this session, so I used EIA's authoritative source directly.

key_facts: SPR crude stood at 325,655 thousand barrels (~325.7 MB) for the week ending June 26, 2026 (EIA WCSSTUS1), the lowest since the early 1980s.
Latest weekly change: -5,536 thousand barrels (-1.7%); year-over-year: -77,110 thousand barrels (-19.1%).
SPR peaked at 415,442 thousand barrels in early-mid March 2026 after the 2025 refill; refilling halted in February 2026 when the US-Iran conflict began.
Continuous drawdown began the week ending March 27, 2026 — about 13 consecutive weeks of releases through June 26.
Record weekly draw of ~9.92 MB occurred in the week ending May 15, 2026; draws ran ~7-10 MB/week from late April through mid-June.
Total SPR decline from the March 2026 peak to June 26 is ~89.8 million barrels (~86 MB since December 2025).
The releases are part of a pledged 172-million-barrel emergency drawdown tied to the US-Iran war / Strait of Hormuz; ~half (~89 MB) had been released by June 26, 2026.
Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced a plan (early June 2026) to refill the SPR by ~40 MB after the conflict ends; no refill is yet visible in the data.
EIA changed the Weekly Petroleum Status Report publication format on June 10, 2026, though underlying data collection is unchanged.
